comment: Each pair: (surface_form_seen_in_text -> canonical_name). The eval seeds the DB with the canonical names + their initial aliases, then asks the resolver to resolve each surface_form. We measure: did it return the right canonical name? Which resolver stage fired?

pairs.comment: trivial exact match — should hit the alias table.
title + same name — normalize() strips honorifics.
last-name only, unique match in DB — auto-accept deterministically (no LLM).
first-name only, unique match — auto-accept (no LLM).

first-name AMBIGUOUS — both Anthony Ha and Anthony Garcia match by subname rule. Resolver refuses to guess without article context: returns null and lets a fresh 'Anthony' Person be created (recoverable via later dedupe), rather than silently picking the wrong canonical.
totally unrelated — must return None (treated as new person).
known person but not in the graph — must NOT be merged into anyone.
